name: Lobster Butter- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 1 Celery stalk, trimmed and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam or boil lobsters until just tender, 8 to 10 minutes.
2. Cool, then remove all lobster meat from shells.
3. Refrigerate meat, covered; set aside.
4. In a large saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons clarified butter over high heat for 3 minutes.
5. Add onion, carrot, celery, garlic, thyme, tarragon, and bay leaf; toss until fragrant.
6. Add lobster shells, stirring for 3 minutes.
7. Add 2 tablespoons Cognac and stir for another 3 minutes.
8. Add butter and tomato paste; simmer gently, covered, for 2 to 3 hours.
9. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a heat-safe container.
10. Press solids firmly with a spatula or spoon to extract as much liquid as possible.
11. Set aside for 15 minutes.
12. Skim any froth from the surface and strain again through several layers of dampened cheesecloth, leaving behind any milky residue in the bottom of the pan (discard residue).
13. Cool completely before chilling or freezing in small containers.
New York's Master Chefs, Bon Appetit Magazine
Written by Richard Sax, Photographs by Nancy McFarland
The Knapp Press, Los Angeles, 1985
Chef: Stanley Kramer, Oyster Bar and Restaurant, Grand Central Station, NYC
